sites_used: https://tnswp.com/DIGIGOV/swp-tnswp.jsp (TN Single Window Portal, HTTP 200, live)

policies.name: Tamil Nadu Industrial Policy 2021 (validity extended; structured package for Large/Mega/Ultra-Mega)
policies.incentive_type: Investor's one-time choice of 4 mutually exclusive Investment Promotion Subsidy options + standard incentives
policies.what_companies_get: Categories: Sub-Large Rs50-300cr, Large Rs300-500cr (150 jobs), Mega Rs500-5000cr (400 jobs), Ultra-Mega >Rs5000cr (2000 jobs); min 50 jobs to qualify. Choice of: (I) 100% SGST reimbursement on final products for 15 years; (II) Fixed Capital Subsidy %EFA by district: A-district Mega 10%/Ultra-Mega 20%; B: 10/12/22%; C: 12/15/25%; (III) Flexible Capital Subsidy up to 35% EFA (A), 37% (B), 40% (C) = 5% standard + boosters for location/employment/exports/ecosystem/sunrise sector; (IV) Turnover-based subsidy for Mega/Ultra-Mega: 1.5-2.0% of turnover (by district and >2000/>4000 jobs), capped at 4% of cumulative EFA/yr for 10 years. Plus: training subsidy Rs4,000/worker/month x6 months (Rs6,000 for women/transgender/PwD/SC-ST), SIPCOT land at 10% concession (A/B) and 50% (C) up to 20% of EFA, 5% interest subvention (Ultra-Mega), electricity tax exemption, stamp duty incentive, SGST refund on capital goods, green industry / quality certification / IP creation incentives
policies.status: In force; official validity-extension GO published (tnip_2021_validity_extension.pdf). No 2025/2026 successor industrial policy on the site - 2021 policy still the headline policy
policies.application_status: TN Single Window Portal (tnswp.com) live and serving; incentives administered via Guidance TN/MoU route for structured packages
policies.site_notes: Full 62-page PDF downloaded from official store and parsed; listed on https://www.investingintamilnadu.com/business-in-tamil-nadu/policy-notifications

policies.name: Tamil Nadu Electric Vehicles Policy 2023
policies.incentive_type: Choice of SGST reimbursement / turnover subsidy / capital subsidy; special battery-ACC subsidy; retrofit incentives
policies.what_companies_get: EV OEMs: 100% gross SGST reimbursement on EVs sold+registered in TN for 15 years (min Rs50cr investment, 50 jobs); OR turnover-based subsidy up to 2% of annual turnover capped at 4% of cumulative EFA/yr for 10 years; OR 15% capital subsidy on EFA in 10 equal annual installments (EV/components/EVSE/charging infra). Battery manufacturing: special 20% capital subsidy in 15 installments; Advanced Chemistry Cell plants: 20% capital subsidy in 10 installments. Retrofitting of ICE commercial vehicles to EV (ARAI-compliant) incentivised up to 31.12.2025; battery recycling projects get battery-manufacturing-level incentives

policies.name: Tamil Nadu R&D Policy 2022
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy + reimbursements for R&D centres
policies.what_companies_get: Special capital subsidy of 25% of EFA disbursed in equal installments over 10 years, ceiling Rs25cr; EFA may include intangible R&D expenditure up to 20% of EFA; 50% reimbursement of national/international certification cost up to Rs1cr; 50% reimbursement of IP (patent/copyright/trademark/GI) expenditure up to Rs1cr (up to Rs5cr for standalone R&D assets); plus TNIP-2021 standard incentives
policies.status: In force; promoted as the operative policy for GCC/R&D investors ('Latest Policies' on the GCC & R&D sector page)
policies.site_notes: 32-page PDF parsed; available in EN/KO/JA/DE/FR on the policy page

policies.name: Tamil Nadu FinTech Policy 2021
policies.incentive_type: Operating-expense, marketing and accelerator reimbursements (startup/firm level)
policies.what_companies_get: 75% reimbursement of operating expenses (rent, power, internet, statutory charges, company registration, R&D/IP costs) up to Rs5 lakh/yr; FinTech Accelerator Kit - free cloud/financial-data/payment-gateway credits up to Rs5 lakh/yr; 50% marketing expense reimbursement; stock-exchange listing fee reimbursement; access to Tamil Nadu Startup Fund of Funds; category expenses non-stackable
policies.status: In force (2021 policy still the listed FinTech policy)
policies.application_status: Back-ended subsidy claims; firm must file undertaking on core TN operations

policies.name: Tamil Nadu Footwear & Leather Products Policy 2022
policies.incentive_type: Turnover-based subsidy or fixed capital subsidy with relaxed eligibility
policies.what_companies_get: Relaxed 'Special Package for FLP Manufacturing' thresholds (e.g. Rs50cr entry vs Rs300cr standard); (a) turnover-based subsidy as % of annual turnover capped at 4% of cumulative EFA/yr for 10 years, or (b) fixed capital subsidy 10% of EFA in 10 equal installments for allied/component units outside clusters (min Rs150cr, 100 jobs); 75% reimbursement of transport/logistics cost for relocating capital goods from abroad (per TNIP 2021); SGST refund on capital goods, interest subvention, electricity tax exemption

policies.name: Tamil Nadu Semiconductor and Advanced Electronics Policy 2024 + Semiconductor Mission 2030
policies.incentive_type: Sector policy + mission GO (details not machine-extractable this run)
policies.what_companies_get: Policy listed on the official policy page in EN/JA/KO/ZH; companion 'Tamil Nadu Semiconductor Mission 2030' GO PDF hosted (semiconductor_mission_go.pdf). Incentive percentages NOT verified this run - the English PDF is served via JS, only translation PDFs appear in static links
policies.site_notes: Existence and official hosting verified; quantitative terms unverified (flagged honestly)

policies.name: Tamil Nadu Electronics Hardware Manufacturing Policy 2020
policies.what_companies_get: Per official sector-wise incentive summary: capital subsidy up to 30% (by location/investment/employment), up to 50% land-lease subsidy in govt parks / C-district private land, 50% stamp duty exemption, training subsidy Rs4,000 (Rs6,000 women)/employee/month x6 months, interest subsidy up to 5% for up to 6 years, 5-year electricity tax exemption, 50% patent and quality-certification subsidies
policies.status: In force (2020 policy still listed; semiconductor/advanced-electronics 2024 policy sits alongside)
policies.site_notes: Numbers from official 'Incentives Offered by GoTN' summary PDF + policy PDF hosted at tn_electronics_hardware_manufacturing_policy.pdf

https://eitbt.karnataka.gov.in/ (Dept of Electronics, IT/BT - GCC/ESDM/IT policy pages; HTTP 200)

policies.name: Karnataka Industrial Policy 2025-30
policies.incentive_type: Investor's choice: capex subsidy OR production/turnover-linked incentive (PLI), with employment and women-workforce boosters - confirms the shift to production-turnover-linked incentives
policies.what_companies_get: Categories: Large Rs50-300cr, Mega Rs300-1000cr, Ultra Mega >Rs1000cr (Ultra Mega min 500 jobs + 6 jobs per additional Rs50cr; employment floor = 50% of investment amount in Rs cr, range 25-500). Choice of (a) Capex subsidy: 25% of Value of Fixed Assets (Zone 1), 20% (Zone 2), 10% (Zone 3, Bengaluru Rural only), disbursed over 5 years post commercial production; or (b) PLI: 2.5% / 2.0% / 1.0% of Net Sales Turnover for 7 years by zone. Total incentives capped at 60% of VFA (30% in Zone 3). Employment boosters +7.5/10/15% on the chosen incentive for 3x-4x/4x-5x/>5x minimum employment (PLI users also get +5% VFA cap); women-workforce boosters +7.5/10/15% at 50/60/70%+ women. Additional: 10% capital subsidy or 10% PLI (+5% VFA) on manufacturing add-ons, stamp duty exemption on loan/lease/sale deeds for first 5 years, existing units qualify with >=25% expansion of fixed assets
policies.status: In force (current policy, supersedes Industrial Policy 2020-25; operative guidelines also published)
policies.application_status: New Single Window System live on investkarnataka.co.in (investor registration, Combined Application Form, Know-Your-Incentives wizard, incentive calculator, UMA chatbot); site FAQ states online incentive-CLAIM module was under development with availability targeted Apr-Jun 2025 quarter; legacy eBiz still live for Affidavit Based Clearances
policies.site_notes: 96-page official PDF downloaded and parsed; policy list also verified at https://investkarnataka.co.in/ka-proposition/investor-friendly-policies/

policies.name: Karnataka Global Capability Centres (GCC) Policy 2024-29 (India's first dedicated GCC policy)
policies.incentive_type: Rental/EPF/opex reimbursements, grants and duty exemptions, weighted to 'Beyond Bengaluru' locations
policies.what_companies_get: For GCCs with min 100 employees in Beyond Bengaluru clusters: rent reimbursement capped at 50% up to Rs2cr; EPF contribution reimbursement up to Rs3,000/employee/month for 2 years; 25% internet-expense reimbursement up to Rs12 lakh for 3 years; electricity duty exemption 5 years + switch from commercial to industrial power tariff; property-tax reimbursement; skilling/new-hire training reimbursement capped 50% up to Rs7cr; R&D infrastructure grants up to 40% or Rs50cr; co-working operator support 75%/50%/25% of vacant-seat cost in years 1-3, max Rs6,000/seat, up to 30 new spaces across 6 clusters; internship stipend reimbursement (1 lakh internships target); Nano GCC (5-50 employees) support; Rs100cr Innovation Fund, AI CoE. Targets: 500 new GCCs (1,000 total), 3.5 lakh jobs, USD 50bn output by 2029
policies.application_status: Administered by Dept of Electronics, IT/BT; policy page live with Benefits/Eligibility tabs; no separate GCC application portal observed
policies.site_notes: 72-page official policy PDF (eitbt.karnataka.gov.in/uploads/media_to_upload1732030878.pdf) downloaded and parsed

policies.name: KESDM Policy 2017-25 (extended) + Special Incentive Scheme for ESDM Sector 2020-25
policies.incentive_type: Capital subsidy, reimbursements and state top-ups for electronics manufacturing/design
policies.what_companies_get: Capital subsidy up to 10% capped Rs10cr for first two anchor units in each greenfield KESDM cluster; 50% prototyping-cost reimbursement up to Rs10 lakh/yr (startups/MSMEs, max 2 approvals/yr); 50% testing/quality-certification reimbursement up to Rs10 lakh/yr; R&D grants up to Rs2cr/yr for registered companies <7 years old (claimable twice per policy period); PF/ESI reimbursement Rs2,000/employee/month for 2 years capped Rs12 lakh/company; industrial (not commercial) power tariff. Eligibility: state value addition rising 25% (Y1) to 45% (Y5); >=50% of workforce in Karnataka; Karnataka registration
policies.status: In force - KESDM Policy formally extended (an 'Extension of KESDM Policy' GO is hosted; page headline shows 'Karnataka ESDM Policy 2017-30'); Special Incentive Scheme 2020-25 remains the named vehicle for electronic components/EMS/semiconductor manufacturing in the Industrial Policy 2025-30 sector table
policies.application_status: KESDM company registration live on the eitbt ESDM vertical; operative guidelines for both the policy and the Special Incentive Scheme hosted
policies.site_notes: Incentive figures read from the official ESDM policy page
